analysis.consistency: High. The spell effect (stabilizing a creature at 0 HP) is consistent with its name and the Necromancy school (in the context of preserving life/preventing death). The mechanics are clearly defined across all standard fields.
analysis.quality: High. It is a very common, foundational, and useful low-level spell. The description is concise and clearly defines its scope (living creature, 0 HP, no effect on undead/constructs).

mechanics.effect: Stabilizes a living creature at 0 hit points.

analysis.notes: Mechanically sound for a standard 5th Edition D&D equivalent cantrip. Its primary function is preventing death from failed death saving throws.
